reasoning:
The value on 2025-06-08 is evaluated against the latest trading index value of 7.25% (as of June 6, 2025). The underlying trend for the ICE BofA US High Yield Index over the last 30 days is steadily downward, with a slope of -0.009 series points per day. A pure linear extrapolation models only a 30-35% chance of the yield crossing back above 7.25% in the next week, suggesting the near-term probability of an increase is quite low.

On a macroeconomic level, the Federal Reserve is broadly expected to hold rates steady at their upcoming June 18th meeting, before initiating roughly 50-75 basis points of rate cuts later in the second half of 2025. This creates structural downward pressure on overall yields. However, widening credit spreads due to potential economic slowdowns or inflation spikes triggered by newer tariff policies add considerable long-term uncertainty. 

Balancing the clear near-term downward momentum (-0.009/day trend) against macro forecasts of lower aggregate rates in late 2025—while explicitly following the forecasting instruction that uncertainty must increase over long time horizons (pulling distant probabilities closer to 0.5)—we generate a monotonically increasing series strictly bound below 0.5.
